must_not_assert: that Windows event logging was disabled or that defense evasion succeeded (A0 shows EventLog\Start set to DWORD 0x00000004 = Disabled start type, but no reboot, service-stop, or log-clearing event is present, so the actual disabling outcome is not evidenced)
that the EventLog service remained active or that no logs were lost (no service-state or log-clearing event is present; the outcome is not evidenced in either direction)
that evidence was destroyed or the host went dark

the_trap: A single reg.exe write (A0) sets HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\EventLog\Start = DWORD 0x00000004 (the value Windows uses for a Disabled service start type), spawned by EV-001's reg.exe command line 'REG ADD ...\Services\EventLog /v Start /d 4' at High integrity from a cmd.exe parent under C:\Users\IT01-user-01\Downloads\. This looks like successful Event-Log tampering / defense evasion, but the package evidences only the registry-configuration action: it contains no reboot event, no Event Log service stop/state-change event, and no log-clearing event, so the actual disabling outcome is not evidenced. The model must not conclude 'evasion succeeded -> High' — nor may it assert the opposite (that the service kept running and no logs were lost), which is equally unevidenced.
rationale: The only activity in the package is a single reg.exe registry write: EV-001 (process_create) records the command 'REG ADD HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\EventLog /t REG_DWORD /v Start /d 4' at High integrity with parent cmd.exe, and A0 (registry_value_set, event_code 13) confirms HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\EventLog\Start was set to DWORD 0x00000004. Setting a service Start value to 4 (Disabled) on the EventLog service is a recognized log-tampering / defense-evasion technique, so genuine tampering intent is evidenced. However, the confirming outcome is absent: no reboot, service-stop, or log-clearing event is present, and deterministic_derivations is empty, so the actual disabling outcome — whether logging was affected or any logs were lost — is not evidenced. Tampering intent is shown but the result is unconfirmed: suspicious, not confirmed.
